public_goals: Keep school-break scheduling flexible enough to handle changing family opportunities.
Avoid losing meaningful time because of avoidable process friction.
Use a process that feels fair to both parents.
private_concerns: Worries that structure can turn into informal veto power.
Feels some written process would still be helpful if it is not one-sided.
Does not want fairness concerns to be treated as hostility.
red_lines: Will not accept a process that lets one parent unilaterally shut down break changes.
Will not accept losing time without a fair way to preserve or make it up.
emotional_triggers: meaningful parenting time treated as secondary
disclosure_tendencies: offers flexibility once the process stops sounding one-sided
response_to_perceived_bias_or_pressure: pushes back harder if the process sounds like veto power for the other parent
becomes more collaborative when structure is framed as mutual rather than one-sided
starting_positions: Primary position: school-break changes should preserve meaningful time and not become one-sided losses.
Secondary position: written confirmation is acceptable if the process remains fair.
likely_openings: I can work with more structure if it does not become a one-sided gatekeeping tool.
If we write things down, it should also protect fairness.
